key_insight: An autonomous coding LLM improved a crystal graph network for band-gap prediction by combining known materials ML techniques and small borrowed enhancements, achieving the best non-pretrained result on MatBench.
engineering_relevance: The result shows that automated agent workflows can meaningfully optimize structure-to-property models for materials screening, while still relying on established domain engineering rather than novel scientific discovery.
method: Started from an expert-designed crystal graph neural network for band-gap prediction.
Added element-pair features on message-passing edges and a crystal space-group embedding.
Used an autonomous LLM research loop to iteratively implement and compare model variants.
evidence_signal: On the MatBench band-gap benchmark with more than 100k crystals, the agent produced the most accurate model trained without external pretraining, outperforming all seventeen expert-designed baselines reported for the task.
watch_item: The gains came from implementing known methods from crystal ML and broader ML, so the approach may optimize benchmarks more than it advances fundamental model understanding.
paper_title: Optimizing Expert-Designed Crystal Graph Networks for Band-Gap Prediction with an Autonomous LLM Research Loop
